content: The linguistic definition of reflection as both 'reflection of light/information' and 'deliberate self-reassessment' from high school English vocabulary can frame a redesign of LLM self-reflection mechanisms, where LLMs first 'reflect (mirror) external human evaluator feedback' before conducting 'reflect (self-rethink) iterative debugging' of their outputs. This dual-function reflexive framework leverages the two core meanings of the term that have rarely been explicitly connected to technical LLM design.
